author: phase-4-coordinator
content: Updated arrow_conv.rs docs to accurately describe what to_arrow actually does: it copies. Removed the misleading 'Zero-copy when the source is C-contiguous' claim from the module doc and the method doc, and removed the bogus 'SAFETY: buffer length is exactly ...' comment that preceded a line of entirely safe code (there was no unsafe). The to_arrow body is unchanged — it still makes one copy via as_slice().to_vec() or to_vec_flat() and then hands the Vec to Arrow's Buffer::from_vec. Cross-references the crate-level zero-copy discussion added in #195. Companion fixes in #548 and #550.
